system_description.overview: Cluely is an AI sales assistant that gives real-time answers, prospect insights, and objection handling during calls. It also provides knowledge base sync, instant meeting briefs, CRM integrations, detailed performance analytics, and enterprise-grade privacy for sales, recruiting, and operations teams.

products.AI Meeting Assistance: Real-time answers, objection handling, follow-up suggestions, live prospect insights during calls, no-bot note taking, AI-generated meeting briefs, auto post-call action items, interactive meeting analytics, pre-call briefs with smart calendar integration
products.Custom Knowledge Base: Sync and manage full library of knowledge, sales content, process docs; 400k+ token context window, RAG-powered retrieval, team-level permissions, usage analytics
products.Coaching and Analytics: Call performance dashboards, missed opportunity tracking, post-call coaching, team/admin reporting
products.White Glove Onboarding & Support: Personalized onboarding, team training, usage analytics, 24/7 direct founder and enterprise support

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Vercel (carve-out — Vercel holds its own SOC 2)
regions: Not explicitly named; Vercel multi-availability zone data centers
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices or servers; fully cloud-hosted on Vercel
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (Vercel high-availability data centers with multiple availability zones)
additional_cloud: AWS (Bedrock — listed as coming soon in network diagram)
Cloudflare Workers (listed in network diagram for Workers, JS, DNS)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Vercel VPC with segmented VPCs and ACLs. Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data by sensitivity and criticality. Customer accounts are appropriately segmented from each other.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Vercel (handles Dashboard, Analytics, Authentication per network diagram)
network_architecture.waf: Vercel (IDS/IPS and firewall capabilities via Vercel platform)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Vercel to identify and protect against threats. Intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) with certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: User requests → Vercel (Dashboard/Analytics/Authentication) → Cloudflare Workers (JS/DNS) → Neon (Data Storage); PostHog for Analytics/Feature Flags; WorkOS for Authentication; AssemblyAI for Transcription; AWS Bedrock (coming soon) for Transcription; OpenAI and Groq for LLMs; Axiom for Monitoring and Alerting

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before migration to production

databases: Neon (PostgreSQL-based serverless database — shown as Data Storage in network diagram)
file_storage: Not explicitly mentioned; likely Vercel/cloud storage
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (CC7.4.2: Backups taken daily, multi-availability zones configured, alerts in place)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: WorkOS (shown as Authentication in network diagram)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required to access source code management tool; two-factor authentication required for employee email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Password policy via Vercel; periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS (Vercel/AWS KMS); access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based (source code management tool with branch protection; MFA required — likely GitHub)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not explicitly named (deployment managed via Vercel pipeline)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to production. Approval process followed prior to merging to master branch.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; critical/security patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Axiom (Monitoring and Alerting shown in network diagram)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Axiom; logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Axiom (automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events)

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure — hosting, serverless compute, authentication, analytics, dashboard

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers and Mac-based workstations/laptops; continuous automatic scanning; definitions updated weekly on production servers
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

untestable: CC2.2.3 — could not be tested because there were no significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 (cybersecurity insurance effectiveness) — could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 (customer data deletion) — could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 (incident response) — could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities; Vercel is responsible for physical security, not Cluely
A1.2 — Environmental protections; Cluely does not have an office; environmental controls managed by Vercel
notable_controls: Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed (CC7.4.2)
BCDR plan tested annually with loss scenario testing (CC7.4.1)
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts (CC6.2.2)
Access revocation within 1 business day of termination (CC6.1.4)
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually; IT Leadership Committee meets weekly
Source code access protected by MFA (CC8.1.3)
Branch protection with required approvals before merging to master (CC8.1.2)

infrastructure.rationale: Vercel-native serverless architecture with multi-AZ and daily backups confirmed. Cloudflare CDN/Workers present. However single cloud provider (Vercel), no explicit multi-region failover, and AWS Bedrock is listed as 'coming soon' suggesting early-stage diversification. IaC in place.

application_architecture.rationale: Modern serverless-heavy pattern on Vercel with clear separation of environments, branch protection, and MFA-gated code commits. Specific languages/frameworks not disclosed. RAG + multi-LLM strategy (OpenAI + Groq) shows architectural sophistication.

data_layer.rationale: Neon (serverless Postgres) confirmed as primary data store, daily backups with multi-AZ. No explicit caching layer, data warehouse, or file storage beyond Neon mentioned. Data classification and retention policies in place.

security_posture.rationale: Strong posture for company age: annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via Vercel, anti-malware on all endpoints, MFA enforced broadly, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews, access revocation within 1 business day. No exceptions in any controls tested.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed, branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews. CI/CD tooling not explicitly named. No container orchestration (serverless-only). Automatic patch management set up by CTO.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, reviewed and tested annually with multiple loss scenarios. Daily backups with multi-AZ. RTO/RPO not disclosed. No multi-region failover mentioned. Solid for an early-stage company.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Rich vendor ecosystem visible in network diagram: Vercel, Neon, WorkOS, AssemblyAI, OpenAI, Groq, Cloudflare, PostHog, Axiom, and AWS Bedrock (coming soon). Multiple LLM providers reduces AI concentration risk. Heavy Vercel dependency is notable single point. 9+ named vendors.

overall.rationale: Strong security program for a short-history SOC 2 Type 2 (only 3-month period). Fully cloud-native on Vercel with a rich vendor ecosystem. Key gaps: RTO/RPO not published, no multi-region DR, specific tech stack not disclosed in report, and the 3-month audit period is short. No exceptions in any of 69 controls tested is a strong positive signal.

red_flags: Very short audit period (3 months: June 27 – September 27, 2025) — limits confidence in control operating effectiveness over time
Entire infrastructure is on a single cloud provider (Vercel); multi-region failover not in place
RTO and RPO not publicly disclosed in the report
AWS Bedrock labeled 'coming soon' in the network diagram — indicates infrastructure is still evolving
yellow_flags: Primary database (Neon) is a relatively early-stage serverless Postgres provider — vendor stability risk
No explicit data warehouse or caching layer mentioned — may indicate limited analytics/performance infrastructure
Specific programming languages and frameworks not disclosed — limits technical due diligence
4 controls could not be tested (no incidents/changes during period) — not exceptions but incomplete operating evidence
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all 69 controls tested in the audit
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party confirmed
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed
BCDR plan documented, reviewed, and tested annually
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
Access revocation within 1 business day of termination
Rich third-party vendor ecosystem with multiple LLM providers (OpenAI + Groq) for redundancy
MFA enforced broadly: email, production platform admin, source code access
Formal Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with IT Leadership Committee meeting weekly
key_observations: Network diagram in report explicitly names all major vendors — unusually transparent for a SOC 2 report; architecture is Vercel-centric with Cloudflare for edge/DNS, Neon for data, WorkOS for auth, multiple AI providers
The 3-month audit period (Jun–Sep 2025) is the company's first SOC 2 Type 2, suggesting rapid compliance maturation — but the short period means limited operating history evidence

Cluely markets itself as a 'desktop AI assistant' but the system description reveals it is primarily a sales intelligence/coaching platform — the system boundary covers an AI-powered real-time meeting assistant with CRM integrations, RAG knowledge base, and post-call analytics
AWS Bedrock listed as 'coming soon' for transcription in the network diagram alongside AssemblyAI, suggesting a planned migration or hybrid approach to audio transcription

Neon (serverless Postgres) as the primary data store is notable — Neon is a modern, fast-scaling option but is a newer vendor compared to established RDS/Aurora options; appropriate for a fast-growing startup

The audit explicitly carves out Vercel's controls (carve-out method) — meaning Vercel's physical security, availability controls and data center operations are not covered; customers must rely on Vercel's own SOC 2
